title: MeshNode Interface Redesign with Typed Ports
description: Redesign the MeshNode interface from its current TerminalOption-style shape (usage/desc/fullName/options) to a geometry-nodes-style interface with typed input/output ports and an evaluate() method. Define port types (MESH, FLOAT, INT, BOOLEAN, VECTOR3, STRING, ROTATION) and a NodeContext that carries evaluation state. Keep @MeshNodeAnnotation(id=...) for registry discovery. Each node declares its ports statically and implements evaluate(NodeContext) which reads inputs and writes outputs. The interface must be clean enough that an LLM can generate new node implementations from a description.
definition-of-done: 1. MeshNode interface in annotations module has: List<InputPort> inputs(), List<OutputPort> outputs(), void evaluate(NodeContext ctx). 2. InputPort and OutputPort classes exist with name, type, default value. 3. PortType enum covers MESH, FLOAT, INT, BOOLEAN, VECTOR3, STRING, ROTATION. 4. NodeContext allows reading typed inputs and writing typed outputs. 5. @MeshNodeAnnotation still works with the annotation processor. 6. At least one example node (e.g., a pass-through or constant node) compiles and evaluates correctly. 7. The old MeshNode methods (usage, desc, etc.) are removed or moved to a separate metadata interface.
testing-plan: 1. Compile the annotations module with the new interface. 2. Create a test node with one FLOAT input and one FLOAT output (double it). 3. Create a NodeContext, set the input, call evaluate, verify output. 4. Verify @MeshNodeAnnotation processor still generates the registry map. 5. Verify port type validation rejects wiring MESH output to FLOAT input.
todos: Define PortType enum in annotations module
Define InputPort record/class with name, type, defaultValue
Define OutputPort record/class with name, type
Define NodeContext interface with getInput(String name, Class<T>), setOutput(String name, Object value)
Redesign MeshNode interface with inputs(), outputs(), evaluate(NodeContext)
Verify MeshNodeAnnotation processor still works with the new interface
Create a trivial example node to validate the contract
Update Icosphere to implement the new interface or remove its @MeshNodeAnnotation temporarily
unknowns: Whether to keep node metadata (display name, description, category) in the annotation or in the interface
Whether NodeContext should support lazy evaluation (pull-based) or eager evaluation (push-based)
